description_long: This object is a tall, cylindrical candle with a smooth, matte beige wax body and a pointed, reddish-brown tip that represents the unlit wick and flame area. The candle's body is uniform in diameter and free from any decorative patterns, wax drips, or embellishments. The top of the candle is flat, with the pointed tip emerging cleanly from the center, suggesting it is unused or only slightly used. There is no visible candle holder or base, and the candle stands upright on its own. The surface appears semi-soft and slightly textured, typical of paraffin or beeswax candles. The overall design is simple and utilitarian, suitable for both decorative and functional use in urban environments.
description: A tall, smooth beige candle with a pointed reddish-brown tip, standing upright without a holder. The surface is clean and free of wax drips or decorations.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full cylindrical beige body and the pointed reddish-brown tip clearly centered.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated, the cylindrical shape and pointed tip remain prominent, with no new features visible.
description_view_2: Another rotated view, maintaining the same smooth body and pointed tip, with consistent proportions.
description_view_3: Final view shows the candle's side, confirming the uniformity of the body and the pointed tip.
